thesis_claim: The most powerful things that help in schizophrenia are the least-discussed — coordinated care and family support rival medication for preventing relapse — and 'non-adherence' is usually a rational response to real side-effect burden, not a failure of will.
the_risk: Two positions in the highest-regulatory-exposure part: psychosocial care rivals medication for relapse; non-adherence is rational. Stated + cited. NEVER a dose, drug recommendation, or 2nd-person treatment imperative.
prescription_drift_guard: ZERO tolerance. Register is 'clinicians often use…', 'the evidence shows…', 'guidelines place…'. Never 'you should…', never a dose, never a named-drug recommendation to the reader. L1 gate scans this part hardest.

block_plan.content: Open on the number that changes expectations: how long psychosis goes untreated predicts long-term outcome (correlations 0.13-0.18, small but consistent) — time, not just illness severity, shapes the course.

stats: longer duration of untreated psychosis -> poorer long-term outcome (r 0.13-0.18)
median untreated period in a first-episode trial: 74 weeks (Kane/RAISE)

block_plan.content: What antipsychotics do and do not do. Comparative efficacy from network meta-analysis: all beat placebo (SMD range -0.89 to -0.03; overall ~0.4), differences between agents gradual. Describe as a class; NO recommendation.
Maintenance vs discontinuation: 1-year relapse 27% on drug vs 64% on placebo (RR 0.40, NNT 3). Report evidence, not advice.

Side-effect burden DESCRIBED never prescribed: metabolic (weight gain up to ~3.2 kg vs placebo), extrapyramidal (EPS OR up to 4.76), tardive dyskinesia (~25% prevalence; 20.7% SGA vs 30.0% FGA). This is WHY the trade-off is a clinician conversation — that framing is the point.

steps: CBT for psychosis — a small adjunct effect (overall -0.33, shrinking to -0.15 in blinded trials); where guidelines place it

Family intervention & psychoeducation — one of the LARGER relapse effects in the whole literature and almost unknown to the public: relapse RR 0.55 (NNT 7), better adherence (RR 0.60). State this asymmetry as such.

after.title: Coordinated specialty / early intervention
after.content: team-based care beat usual care on all 13 outcomes (Correll 2018); RAISE improved quality of life, symptoms, work/school (Kane 2016)

block_plan.content: The institutionalisation folk-picture is outdated; ECT's actual role is narrow (augmentation for antipsychotic-non-responsive cases, very-weak evidence, substantial caution) — not the popular image.
THE RISK, stated + cited: 'non-adherence' (44-56%) framed as moral failure vs as a rational response to real side-effect burden and impaired insight. Take the position.
